us.label: US Wedding
us.venue: The Stone Mill at New York Botanical Garden

timeline.label: Vendor Access to Venue
timeline.description: Vendors may enter the venue for setup and preparation
timeline.details: All vendors (catering, florals, AV, coordination) may arrive for setup. Please check in at the staff entrance on Southern Blvd. Load-in through the service corridor. Event coordination team will be onsite to direct you.

timeline.label: Couple Access to Venue for Photography
timeline.description: Bride, groom, and immediate family arrive for pre-ceremony photos
timeline.details: The couple and immediate family have access to the venue for private photography before guest arrival. First look and family formals will be captured at the Stone Mill and Conservatory. Please arrive promptly at 3:00pm — the photographer's schedule is tight.

timeline.label: Guest Arrival
timeline.description: Guests arrive and are seated for the ceremony
timeline.details: Please arrive and make your way to the Stone Mill for the ceremony. Ushers will guide you to your seat. This is a great time to sign the guest book and find your seat before the ceremony begins at 6:30pm sharp.

timeline.label: Ceremony
timeline.description: Join us under the arbor as we exchange our vows
timeline.details: The ceremony will take place at the Stone Mill, weather permitting. In case of rain, we'll move to the Conservatory (indoor). Please arrive by 6:15 PM to find your seat. Phones are welcome but we ask that you stay seated and refrain from blocking the aisle.

timeline.label: Cocktail Hour
timeline.description: Hors d'oeuvres and drinks in the garden courtyard
timeline.details: Enjoy passed hors d'oeuvres featuring seasonal ingredients from the garden's own farm. A selection of wine, beer, and our signature cocktail will be served. This is a great time to explore the garden courtyard and take photos with the golden hour light.

timeline.label: Reception
timeline.description: Dinner, toasts, and dancing under the stars
timeline.details: A plated dinner will be served followed by heartwarming toasts from our closest family and friends. After dinner, the dance floor opens! We've curated a playlist spanning classics, Motown, and modern hits. The bar will remain open throughout. Late-night snacks will appear around 10 PM.

timeline.label: Vendor Load Out
timeline.description: Vendors may return for teardown and equipment pickup
timeline.details: Vendors may enter for teardown and load-out after the reception concludes. All equipment, decor, and rentals must be removed by 1:00am. Please check out with the coordination team before departing.

us.dressCode: Formal / Black-tie optional — garden party attire welcome, just no white please

transport.car: The New York Botanical Garden is located at 2900 Southern Blvd, Bronx, NY. There are several parking garages nearby.
transport.train: Metro-North Railroad from Grand Central to Botanical Garden Station. The journey takes approximately 20 minutes.
transport.subway: Take the 2 or 5 train to Pelham Parkway station, then a short taxi or bus ride to the garden.
transport.air: The closest airports are LaGuardia (LGA) and JFK International (JFK). Newark Liberty (EWR) is also accessible via train or car. A taxi or ride-share from LGA is about 15-20 minutes, from JFK about 30-40 minutes.

india.label: India Wedding
india.address: 63 1st Seaward Road, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600041, India

timeline.label: Mehendi
timeline.description: Mehendi ceremony for Rebecca, Paulette, Jayashree, and Abhay
timeline.details: A joyful pre-wedding ceremony where intricate henna designs are applied. Join us for music, mingling, and celebration as we kick off the wedding festivities.

timeline.label: Virutham, Nischayathaartham & Mehendi
timeline.description: A day of pre-wedding rituals: Virutham, formal engagement, and mehendi mingling
timeline.details:
The Viratham ceremony will be conducted on the morning of February 24th. Viratham (or Vrutham) is a significant pre-wedding ritual in Tamil Hindu weddings, particularly among Brahmins, symbolizing the transition from bachelorhood/spinsterhood to married life (Grihastha). It involves fasting and prayers by the bride, groom, and their parents to seek ancestral blessings for a harmonious, long-lasting marriage.

The Nischayathaartham is the formal engagement ceremony where the families come together to finalize the wedding commitments, followed by mehendi and mingling.

timeline.label: Muhurtham
timeline.description: The main wedding ceremony: Kaasi Yathirai, Garland exchange, Oonjal, Kanya daanam, Pani grahanam & Sapthapathi
timeline.details:
Once the couple announces their engagement, the Priest, along with the couple's parents, come together to discuss the Muhurtham. Based on the couple's horoscopes, the Priest determines the most auspicious day to conduct the marriage and time to start ceremony. The calculation is derived from Hindu Vedic Astrology and is of utmost importance in order to avoid the presence of evil eye at the wedding.

The Muhurtham ceremony includes:

• Kaasi Yathirai — The groom pretends to leave for Kashi (a holy city) to become an ascetic, and the bride's father persuades him to return and embrace married life
• Garland exchange — The bride and groom exchange garlands, symbolizing mutual acceptance
• Oonjal — The couple sits on a decorated swing as women sing traditional songs
• Kanya daanam — The bride's father gives her away to the groom
• Pani grahanam — The groom holds the bride's hand, sealing the union
• Sapthapathi — The seven sacred steps around the holy fire, each representing a vow

timeline.label: Wedding Reception
timeline.description: Grand dinner reception with celebration, music, and traditional cuisine
timeline.details: Join us for a festive evening of dinner, music, and celebration following the wedding ceremony. A feast of traditional Indian cuisine will be served.

india.dressCode:
Traditional Indian attire encouraged. Women typically wear Sarees for the ceremony and Lehengas for the reception. Men usually wear a Kurta or Sherwani with dress pants. Since the ceremony is religious, please dress modestly (covered shoulders and longer hemlines). Please avoid the colors black and red. If you are part of the Wedding Party, please coordinate with Rebecca and Abhay.

Rental options: Vitham Rentals (Chennai), Aayka Fashion (NYC — free pickup), All Borrow (online rental).

transport.car: Chennai is well-connected by road. Dwaraka Palace is located at 63 1st Seaward Road, Chennai, Tamil Nadu 600041. Taxis and ride-sharing services (Uber, Ola) are widely available.
transport.train: Chennai Central Railway Station (MAS) is approximately 8 km from the venue. Many auto-rickshaws and taxis are available outside the station.
transport.subway: The nearest Chennai Metro station is Thirumayilai. From there, an auto-rickshaw or taxi is a short ride to the venue.
transport.air: Chennai International Airport (MAA) is about 15 km from the venue. Taxis and ride-sharing services (Uber, Ola) are readily available at the airport. The drive to Dwaraka Palace takes approximately 30-45 minutes depending on traffic.
